what_it_does: Defines an MCP tool wrapper `cc_retire` that exposes three retirement operations—status, member, and channel—validating required arguments and delegating all work to a runtime `cc_retire` module.
observations: Tool is registered via `@mcp.tool()` inside `register(mcp, suite)`
Delegates all work to `runtime.cc_retire`, imported lazily inside the tool function
Requires explicit `archive=true` to archive a channel; default is false
Claim statuses are described as verified/attempted-unverified/broken/abandoned and enforced by the runtime
The module docstring frames retirement as crystallizing perspective or totality into one honest corpus record
standouts: The honest-state claim taxonomy: no self-certified done, only verified/attempted-unverified/broken/abandoned
Explicit opt-in archival to prevent accidental channel closure
Lazy import of the runtime module inside the tool function rather than at module top
concerns: Lazy import hides import-time problems until first invocation
No local validation of `claims` item structure beyond requiring a non-empty list; runtime must enforce status values and shape
Default empty strings for required fields rely on runtime or caller to supply values
notes: A thin, policy-enforcing MCP adapter that turns session or channel wind-down into a repeatable, coverage-checked harvest verb.
questions: What interface does `suite.store` expose and how is it initialized?
What exact schema does `runtime.cc_retire` expect for claims and what does it return?
How does the MCP server discover and invoke this `register` function?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file exposes the three documented retirement operations, validates required arguments, and delegates to the runtime implementation.
intention_signals: To prevent sessions or channels from closing without a traceable, attributed, and honest record of what was learned, attempted, or left unresolved.
novelty: Treats session/channel close as a knowledge-crystallization ritual with an enforced claim-state taxonomy and coverage checking.
contribution: Provides a reusable MCP-facing retirement verb that bridges server tooling with the underlying runtime retirement logic while surfacing operation semantics and required parameters.
summary_for_embedding: MCP tool file that registers `cc_retire` with status, member, and channel operations, validates inputs, and delegates to `runtime.cc_retire` for harvest and retirement logic.
intention_for_embedding: To make winding down a member session or channel a repeatable, honest-state, coverage-checked harvest into a single corpus record.

symbols.does: Installs the `cc_retire` MCP tool on the server.
symbols.description: The `register` function receives an MCP instance and a suite object, then decorates `cc_retire` with `@mcp.tool()` so the retirement verb becomes available to the MCP server.

symbols.does: Handles status, member, and channel retirement operations by delegating to `runtime.cc_retire`.
symbols.description: The tool function validates the requested `op` and required arguments, obtains the suite store, and calls the corresponding function in `runtime.cc_retire` to produce a status report, member harvest, or channel retirement record.
